commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then she that is my enemy shall see it, and shame shall cover her who said unto me, Where is the LORD your God. my eyes shall behold her: now shall she be trodden down as the mire of the streets. She; Babylon, my enemy. (Challoner) It was taken by the Medes and Persians, who set the Jews at liberty, to the great mortification of their enemies. (Worthington) God thus displayed his justice or mercy, rescuing his people from the night of misery. Cyrus treated the fallen city with contempt. It stood for some time afterwards.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: She shall be covered: Viz., Babylon my enemy.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
